meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.
meta.blueprintRefinement: THIS BLUEPRINT MUST BE CONTINUALLY REFINED. After completing any task, before starting the next, re-read this file and assess whether it still accurately captures reality. If any task description is stale, wrong, unclear, or missing context — FIX IT NOW. The blueprint is a living document. A stale blueprint is worse than no blueprint. Refine aggressively: split tasks that are too coarse, merge tasks that are redundant, rewrite descriptions so a cold-start reader can act on them, and add tasks you discover along the way.

globalAlerts.warning: ON TOOLS: Use config.mk to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that limit the amount of output you receive. Pipe through tail (with FEW lines output) after tee to build.log at path ./build.log (gitignored). Use 'make find-test-failures' to check for failures.

NAMING: The word 'orchestrator' is RETIRED as a module/package name. The correct name is 'claudemux'. If you see 'orchestrator' in code or docs, it MUST be corrected. The JS module is 'osm:claudemux', the Go package is 'claudemux', the directory is internal/builtin/claudemux/.
FEEDBACK CHANNEL: MCP is the PRIMARY means by which Claude Code instances send structured data back to OSM. PTY is for session lifecycle ONLY.

CLI ENTRY POINT: The CLI wrapper wraps ollama launch claude, NOT just claude. The full invocation path involves: (1) ollama launch claude --config, (2) a TUI model selection menu that MUST be navigated programmatically over PTY, (3) then the Claude Code session itself. The model selection menu is a CRITICAL PATH.

CROSS-PLATFORM: ALL checks must pass on ALL three platforms: macOS (make-all-with-log), Linux (make-all-in-container via Docker), Windows (make-all-run-windows via host 'moo'). Zero tolerance for failures on ANY platform.
TIMING: Tests MUST NOT be timing-dependent. Long-running tests are BANNED. Race detector must pass (-race flag). Abide. OBEY.
SESSION TIMER: Session started 2026-02-18T14:49:00Z. 9-hour mandate ends 2026-02-18T23:49:00Z. Verify with 'make check-session-time'.
RULE OF TWO: Two contiguous, issue-free subagent review cycles are REQUIRED before any commit. Each review covers: diff vs HEAD, all platform checks, lint, tests. No exceptions.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are designed to be executed in isolation. Do not attempt to solve all tasks in a single turn. One task at a time, in order.
mandatoryDirectives.subagentPolicy: Use subagents for the Rule of Two review gate ONLY. Work subagents do NOT self-review. TOP-LEVEL ORCHESTRATOR owns the gate.
mandatoryDirectives.blueprintRefinement: Continual blueprint refinement is required. After every task completion, re-read the blueprint and fix stale descriptions. Add discovered work. Split coarse tasks.
mandatoryDirectives.runMakeAfterEveryChange: Run 'make make-all-with-log' (via config.mk target) after EVERY code change. Check 'make find-test-failures'. Zero failures required before proceeding.

statusSection.currentState: Cross-platform baseline COMPLETE. All three platforms green (macOS, Linux via Docker, Windows via moo). Fixes: exec_test.go ETXTBSY, filelock_windows.go best-effort Remove, gitops.go PullRebase stdout+stderr conflict detection. Next task: P006 (DS_Store audit), then P007 (module resolution docs).
statusSection.completedPriorWork: T001-T005 (races/PTY/make), T011-T016 (eventloop/gRPC/fetch/AbortSignal), T029-T096 (osm:flag, exec ctx, git diff, config persist, prompt-flow, hot-snippets, tview removal, cleanup sched, logging, etc.), T104-T106 (cross-platform, sync), T126-T127 (~/.osm, which-is-better), T135-T136 (deadcode/betteralign), T164 (CHANGELOG)

sequentialTasks.task: P001: macOS baseline verification
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make make-all-with-log'. Check 'make find-test-failures'. Zero failures required. This is the baseline gate for all subsequent work.

sequentialTasks.task: P002: Session timer initialization
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make session-timer-init' to write .session-timer. Verify with 'make check-session-time'. The 9-hour mandate requires this file to persist. If the file is lost, the clock restarts from zero.

sequentialTasks.task: P003: Create WIP.md
sequentialTasks.description: Write WIP.md capturing: current branch state (wip, 251 commits ahead of main), session timer reference, immediate next task, key file paths, and any stack state for the next Takumi to resume from cold start.

sequentialTasks.task: P004: Linux baseline verification
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make make-all-in-container'. This uses Docker with golang container to run all checks on Linux. Verify with 'make find-test-failures'. Zero failures required. If failures found, fix before proceeding.
sequentialTasks.completionNote: Fixed ETXTBSY in internal/builtin/exec/exec_test.go (multi-element array test uses osexec.LookPath('echo') instead of shell script). Linux all-green.

sequentialTasks.task: P005: Windows baseline verification
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make make-all-run-windows'. This uses hack/run-on-windows.sh to run all checks on Windows host 'moo'. Verify with 'make find-test-failures'. Zero failures required. If failures found, fix before proceeding.
sequentialTasks.completionNote: Fixed filelock_windows.go best-effort Remove (silences SHARING_VIOLATION/ACCESS_DENIED). Fixed PullRebase in gitops.go to capture stdout for conflict detection and use case-insensitive matching. Windows all-green.

sequentialTasks.task: P006: T178 - .DS_Store hygiene audit
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make check-ds-store'. If any .DS_Store files are tracked, run 'make remove-ds-store' and commit removal. Verify .gitignore contains '.DS_Store'. Currently confirmed: no tracked files. Mark done.

sequentialTasks.task: P007: T166 - Module resolution documentation
sequentialTasks.description: Document the require() algorithm: read internal/scripting/module_hardening.go, document path traversal protection, script.module-paths config key behavior, ESM feasibility in Goja (document limitations). Update docs/scripting.md or add docs/reference/module-resolution.md. Verify docs are accurate against code. Run make.
sequentialTasks.completionNote: Added 'Module security (path-traversal protection)' and 'ES module syntax (ESM) limitations' sections to docs/scripting.md, accurately documenting all three security layers (path resolver check, symlink escape check, startup validation) and ESM non-support in Goja.

sequentialTasks.task: P008: T031 - Rewrite architecture doc as claude-mux
sequentialTasks.description: REWRITE docs/architecture-ai-orchestrator.md FROM SCRATCH as docs/architecture-claude-mux.md. Cover: two-channel architecture (PTY + MCP), full invocation path (ollama launch claude -> model selection TUI -> Claude Code), dynamic MCP config per instance, MCP feedback protocol (reportProgress/reportResult/requestGuidance/reportError), guard rails, PTY spawning, multi-instance session isolation, security model, BT integration patterns, integration testing approach. Include Mermaid diagrams. NEVER use 'orchestrator'. DELETE the old file. Run make.
sequentialTasks.completionNote: Created docs/architecture-claude-mux.md with two-channel architecture, full invocation path, MCP protocol, security model, session isolation, platform support, roadmap. Deleted docs/architecture-ai-orchestrator.md. Added to docs/README.md.

sequentialTasks.task: P009: T032 - Rename orchestrator package to claudemux
sequentialTasks.description: Rename internal/builtin/orchestrator/ to internal/builtin/claudemux/. Steps: (1) Rename directory. (2) Change all 'package orchestrator' to 'package claudemux'. (3) Update import path in internal/builtin/register.go and all other importers. (4) Change JS module name 'osm:orchestrator' to 'osm:claudemux' in module.go. (5) Update all test files in the package. (6) Update internal/builtin/register_test.go. (7) Update scripts/bt-templates/orchestrator.js require() calls. Run 'make make-all-with-log' and fix any issues.

sequentialTasks.task: P010: T034 - Rename BT templates file to claude-mux.js
sequentialTasks.description: Rename scripts/bt-templates/orchestrator.js to scripts/bt-templates/claude-mux.js. Update templatePath() in templates_test.go to point to 'claude-mux.js'. Update file header comment. Update scripts/README.md. Update docs/architecture-claude-mux.md reference. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P011: T033 - Model selection TUI parser extension
sequentialTasks.description: Extend parser.go in claudemux package to detect ollama model selection menu: detect available models list (e.g. lines like '> gpt-oss:20b-cloud'), parse selected model, generate navigation keystrokes to select a specific model (arrow keys + enter). Handle: single model, model not found, navigation timeout. Add modelSelect event fields: {modelName, availableModels, selectedIndex}. Add tests with representative PTY output samples. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P012: T035 - Rewrite PR split goal and script
sequentialTasks.description: Rewrite goals/orchestrate-pr-split.json for claude-mux with proper stateVars, hotSnippets, flagDefs, promptOptions. Rewrite scripts/orchestrate-pr-split.js: use require('osm:claudemux'), implement conflict handling via Claude, add equivalence verification step. Update tests (package now 'claudemux'). Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P013: T062 - MCP feedback protocol
sequentialTasks.description: Add new MCP tools to internal/command/mcp.go: reportProgress(sessionId, step, message, percent), reportResult(sessionId, resultType, data), requestGuidance(sessionId, question, options), reportError(sessionId, errorType, message, recoverable). JSON schemas. Idempotent via sequence numbers. Heartbeat mechanism. Documentation. Tests including fuzz tests for schema validation. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P014: T061 - Dynamic MCP server config per Claude instance
sequentialTasks.description: Per-instance MCP lifecycle: unique endpoint URL per instance (Unix socket or TCP random port), startup sequencing (MCP ready before Claude spawn), config generation (write JSON config for Claude's MCP client to temp location), shutdown cleanup (remove temp config, kill orphaned MCP). Config validation before spawn. Tests with mocks for startup/shutdown cycle. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P015: T066 - Session isolation for multi-instance
sequentialTasks.description: Unique session ID per Claude instance, per-instance MCP endpoint (from P014), isolated state dirs under ~/.osm/claude-sessions/<id>/, independent PTY handles, resource cleanup on Close(), session registry (concurrent map). No shared mutable state between instances. Tests with -race flag. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P016: T063 - Guard rails harness
sequentialTasks.description: PTY monitors: rate-limit detection (pause + backoff), permission prompt (fail-closed reject), crash detection (restart or escalate), timeout. MCP monitors: no-calls timeout, excessive frequency, repeated calls, unexpected tools, schema violations. Remediation: warn/pause/restart/kill/escalate. Composable threshold config (claude-mux.guard.*). Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P017: T065 - Error recovery and cancellation
sequentialTasks.description: PTY error detection (EOF, crash, unexpected exit). MCP error detection (timeout, malformed response). Recovery strategies: force-kill + restart (configurable max retries), escalate to caller. Cancellation: propagate context cancellation. Graceful shutdown: drain output, send final MCP notification, close PTY. Tests covering all recovery paths. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P018: T068 - Concurrent instance management
sequentialTasks.description: Instance pool with configurable size. Task queue (buffered channel). Health tracking per instance. Load balancing (round-robin or least-loaded). Instance recycling after N tasks or on error. Graceful drain on shutdown. Go API: Pool struct with Submit(task), Drain(), Close(). Tests with mocks. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P019: T064 - TUI multiplexing
sequentialTasks.description: Multi-instance TUI panel: meta-key switching (Alt/Escape prefix, NOT Ctrl-B to avoid tmux conflict), state save/restore, status bar with health indicators, input routing, output rendering with scrollback per session. BubbleTea integration. Tests for input routing and state management. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P020: T067 - Expose claude-mux building blocks as JS API
sequentialTasks.description: Expand osm:claudemux module (module.go): instance pool management, MCP helpers (waitForResult, getProgress), BT action template access, parser patterns list, custom provider interface from JS, guard rails config from JS. Update scripts/ examples. Update docs/scripting.md. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P021: T069 - Claude Code wrapper/parser JS native module
sequentialTasks.description: Expand osm:claudemux: createSession(opts)->handle, sendTask(sessionId, prompt, opts)->Promise, onProgress(sessionId, cb), onResult(sessionId, cb), onGuidanceRequest(sessionId, cb), parseOutput(line)->event, close(sessionId). Register in register.go. Tests covering full JS API surface. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P022: T070 - Safety validation
sequentialTasks.description: Intent classification (code change / destructive / network / credential), scope assessment (file vs repo), risk scoring (destructive potential + scope + reversibility + external deps), policy enforcement (block/warn/require-confirmation). Composable validator interface. Optional Ollama integration (localhost:11434). Config keys: claude-mux.safety.*. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P023: T071 - Ideal choice resolution
sequentialTasks.description: Multi-candidate analysis with criteria, criteria weighting (correctness, efficiency, maintainability, safety), recommendation with justification, user confirmation hook via requestGuidance MCP tool. Optional Ollama integration. Config keys: claude-mux.choice.*. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P024: T072 - Main claude-mux entry point command
sequentialTasks.description: Create 'osm claude-mux' command in internal/command/. Config keys: claude-mux.*. Wire together: instance pool (P018), MCP feedback (P013), guard rails (P016), session isolation (P015), error recovery (P017). Audit logging. Fail-closed on errors. Register as built-in in registry.go. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P025: T101 - Integration testing for claude-mux
sequentialTasks.description: CRITICAL: TestMain + -integration flag (disabled by default). Flags: -provider=ollama, -model=gpt-oss:20b-cloud. Test: PTY spawn of 'ollama launch claude --config', navigate model menu to select 'gpt-oss:20b-cloud', MCP startup, send test task, verify result via MCP, test guard rails (inject synthetic rate-limit), test error recovery (kill PTY). Config.mk target 'make integration-test'. Skipped when flag unset. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P026: T162 - Sync common config enhancement
sequentialTasks.description: Enhance sync.go/sync_config.go: conflict handling (prompt user for unknown-state files, auto-override for SHA-tracked files), schema versioning in YAML frontmatter, edge cases (empty repo, partial failure, concurrent sync), gitignored file handling (detect and warn). Tests for each new behavior. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P027: T163 - Autodiscovery refinement
sequentialTasks.description: Verify 'osm goal paths' and 'osm script paths'. Test: config key respect, symlink traversal, permission error handling (warn not crash), performance with many paths (benchmark), debug logging via goal.debug-discovery and script.debug-discovery. Add missing edge case tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P028: T167 - Prompt file enhancement
sequentialTasks.description: Verify .prompt.md discovery via prompt.file-paths config in prompt_file.go. Test: goal references, VS Code conventions, composition, templating. Add missing tests. Update docs/reference/config.md with prompt.file-paths documentation. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P029: T014 - ReadableStream for Response.body streaming
sequentialTasks.description: Add ReadableStream to Response.body in internal/builtin/fetch/. getReader() returns ReadableStreamDefaultReader. reader.read() returns Promise<{value: Uint8Array, done: boolean}>. Support SSE, chunked encoding. Back-pressure via goroutine. Tests with httptest.Server sending chunked/SSE responses. Verify works from Goja JS. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P030: T173 - macOS process hierarchy fallback for session ID
sequentialTasks.description: Implement Darwin Deep Anchor walk in session_darwin.go (//go:build darwin): sysctl/libproc for process tree, skip ephemeral wrappers, find shell boundaries (bash, zsh, fish), root boundaries (login, sshd, launchd), PID + start-time for recycling safety. Update session_other.go build tag. Update session docs. Tests for persistence using same anchor process. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P031: T168 - Anthropic prompt library expansion
sequentialTasks.description: Add 3-5 new built-in goals to goal_builtin.go. Review https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/resources/prompt-library/library for best candidates not yet implemented. Each goal: stateVars, hotSnippets, flagDefs, promptOptions. Test: metadata, stateVars, promptOptions, commands, hotSnippets, postCopyHint, uniqueness, JSON roundtrip, list output. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P032: T102 - Security audit of scripting sandbox
sequentialTasks.description: Audit: (1) no ambient authority leaks at runtime init, (2) require() path traversal (test ../../etc/passwd etc), (3) osm:exec command injection vectors, (4) osm:fetch SSRF (localhost/169.254.169.254/internal IPs), (5) osm:pty spawn restrictions, (6) osm:os writeFile/appendFile path restrictions, (7) env var secret exposure, (8) MCP tool privilege escalation via args. Review security_test.go, security_sandbox_test.go, security_input_test.go. Fix vulnerabilities. Add regression tests. Update docs/security.md. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P033: txtar context root formatting fix
sequentialTasks.description: Fix internal/scripting/context.go ToTxtar(): context root path must be placed OUTSIDE the txtar code block, wrapped in backticks (`path/to/root`), not embedded inside. Write a failing test first demonstrating current behavior. Fix. Verify test passes. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P034: txtar path ambiguity fix
sequentialTasks.description: Fix computeUniqueSuffixes() in context.go: (1) Compute and include LCA of all tracked paths in txtar header comment. (2) When expanding paths upward for disambiguation, verify implied parent directory is actually tracked. (3) Preserve full paths for non-colliding basenames that appear to share a directory. Write failing tests demonstrating all three scenarios from docs/todo.md. Fix. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P035: T170 - osm mcp make - port of mcp-server-make
sequentialTasks.description: Implement 'osm mcp make' subcommand. Research github.com/joeycumines/mcp-server-make. Integrate with command registry + MCP server. Improvements: better error messages, target caching (parse Makefile once per invocation), cross-platform make detection (gmake vs make), session integration (use session working dir). MCP compliance. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P036: T171 - Agent steering via MCP enqueue + session gRPC
sequentialTasks.description: Session-tied Unix socket (gRPC transport), Windows TCP fallback. Proto definitions for enqueue protocol (task, interrupt, status). Port MacosUseSDK baseline patterns. MCP tools: enqueue (add task), interrupt (stop current task), status query. Socket cleanup on session exit. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P037: T172 - osm mcp parent - Claude Code wrapper + stdio MCP + session socket
sequentialTasks.description: Implement 'osm mcp parent' subcommand: wrap Claude Code as child process, stdio MCP transport (expose MCP tools to Claude via stdin/stdout), session socket bidirectional messaging (from P036), lifecycle management (start/monitor/restart). MCP tools to Claude: enqueue, interrupt, status. Session integration. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P038: T169 - osm mcp prompt - polymorphic terminal wrapper
sequentialTasks.description: Create 'osm mcp prompt' subcommand. Research Anthropic computer use interface. Document research in docs/ first. Dynamic tool availability per mode, BubbleTea terminal UI, mode detection, go-prompt REPL integration, MCP tool schema adapts per mode. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P039: T174 - Sync go-git v6 migration
sequentialTasks.description: Migrate sync.go and sync_startup.go from shell-out git to go-git v6 for: clone, add, commit. Keep pull --rebase as shell-out. Preserve: pull --rebase strategy, conflict detection from 'CONFLICT' in stderr, sync.local-path config, auto-pull on startup, timestamp commit messages 'osm sync: <RFC3339>'. Tests verifying all preserved behaviors. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P040: CHANGELOG update batch 1
sequentialTasks.description: Update CHANGELOG.md with all changes implemented from P006 through P039. Follow Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0 format. Group: Added, Changed, Fixed, Removed. Use present-tense imperative. No PR references. Stays [Unreleased]. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: P041: T177 - Final cross-platform verification
sequentialTasks.description: Run all three: (1) macOS: make make-all-with-log. (2) Linux: make make-all-in-container. (3) Windows: make make-all-run-windows. Check 'make find-test-failures' for each. Zero failures required on ALL platforms. Fix any platform-specific regressions. This is the FINAL quality gate.

sequentialTasks.task: P042: Final CHANGELOG update and commit
sequentialTasks.description: Final CHANGELOG.md update. Verify two contiguous issue-free Rule of Two review cycles against full diff vs main. Execute Rule of Two: spawn subagent, review diff, check all platforms pass, no lint errors. Second subagent, same diff. Only after TWO consecutive PASSes: commit all staged changes with Kubernetes-style message.

continuousVerification.task: Run 'make make-all-with-log' after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description: Execute after: (a) Each code change, (b) Each test addition, (c) Each task completion, (d) Each bug fix. Check build.log via 'make find-test-failures'.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix before proceeding. Zero tolerance.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: Target 100% effective test coverage. VERIFY with go tool cover.
continuousVerification.crossPlatform: Run make-all-in-container (Linux) and make-all-run-windows (Windows) at P004/P005 and again at P041. All three platforms must pass.

fileCorrectionMandate.purpose: These files are WRONG and MUST BE CORRECTED.

files.problem: Wrong name. Must be REWRITTEN as docs/architecture-claude-mux.md.
Wrong name. Must be RENAMED to claude-mux.js.
Package name retired. Must be renamed to claudemux.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ON TOOLS: Use config.mk custom targets. Limit output (pipe to tail). Tee to build.log. Use make find-test-failures.
Timing dependent tests are BANNED. Long-running tests are BANNED. Abide. OBEY.
The word 'orchestrator' is RETIRED. Use 'claude-mux' (user-facing), 'claudemux' (Go identifiers), 'osm:claudemux' (JS module name).
Continually refine. After every task, re-read. Fix stale descriptions. Add discovered work. Split coarse tasks. Never prune unless genuinely done.
Two contiguous, issue-free subagent reviews BEFORE any commit. Both reviews examine the SAME diff. If code changes between reviews, reset counter to 0.
The 9-hour mandate is enforced. If .session-timer is missing, the clock restarts from zero. 'make check-session-time' to verify.
P041 (final cross-platform) MUST pass on ALL three platforms before work is considered complete.
